title: cissus subrhomboidea
intro: Cissus subrhomboidea, also known as Bone setter or Veldt grape, is a perennial climber plant native to South Africa, belonging to the Vitaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, due to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and ascorbic acid. The health benefits of Cissus subrhomboidea include its ability to promote bone health, reduce inflammation, and improve joint mobility. Herbal preparations of Cissus subrhomboidea include teas, capsules, and extracts, which can be used to support bone health and reduce the risk of osteoporosis.
